meta.purpose: Parallelizable v6 implementation tasks for a second Claude instance to execute independently, each in its own branch off `v6`. Pick the hardest UNBLOCKED task (lowest pick_order whose depends_on are all merged). One branch per task; PR back into v6.
meta.worktree_note: Create each task's worktree OUTSIDE the repo tree (e.g. C:/Users/john/projects/worktrees/<owner>_<repo>_<type>_<yy-mm-dd>_<branch>). Never nest a worktree under the repo — it double-loads TypeDoc and breaks the build.

naming_conventions.val: machine-level typed MUTABLE extended-state variable (megaspec §5). The keyword is `val`, NOT `var` (the phase-1 PLAN file predates the rename and says `var`/JssmVarType/.var() throughout — implement everything as `val`/JssmValType/.val()). Using `val` also avoids the reserved-word problem a method named `var` would hit.
naming_conventions.prop: typed (optionally) per-machine/per-state property. In CODE today the pipeline is still named `property_definition` / `_property_keys` / `known_props` — the property->prop rename is NOT yet applied to code; mirror the existing `property` pipeline names when adding parallel tracks, do not rename property yet.

project_rules: NO COMPOUND COMMANDS: never join shell commands with && || ; or a pipe. Run `git add` and `git commit` as separate commands. (npm scripts that contain && internally are fine.)
git verb-first: never put an option between `git` and its subcommand (no `git -C`). cd as its own command to act elsewhere.
npm verb-first: never put an option between `npm` and its subcommand. Flags go after the verb.
DO NOT bump package version during tasks. The version bump + full build happen once via /sc-commit on the branch before the PR.

100% spec-coverage gate: vitest-spec requires 100% line/branch coverage over src/ts/** (excluding the generated fsl_parser). Every branch you add must be exercised by a spec test. Do NOT write unreachable branches (e.g. a default: on an exhaustive discriminated union) — they cannot be covered and fail the gate.
No fake tests, no golden/snapshot tests, no bug-pinning. Tests validate real parse->construct->accessor behavior; assert thrown error types and message substrings, not whole-object snapshots.
Stochastic tests go in *.stoch.ts (vitest.stoch.config.ts); spec tests in *.spec.ts (vitest.spec.config.ts). They report coverage independently — keep them separate.
Grammar: edit src/ts/fsl_parser.peg, then run `npm run peg` to regenerate src/ts/fsl_parser.ts. NEVER hand-edit the generated fsl_parser.ts.
After editing .ts files, check IDE diagnostics (mcp__ide__getDiagnostics) before considering a task done.
Build before merge: `npm run build` (not `npm run make`) regenerates every tracked artifact; a make-only tree is an invalid release state.
main and v6 release on push — never push to v6 directly; open a PR. Ask before merging into v6.
Durable docs live in notes/ (npm run clean does rm -rf docs/).
meta.tech_stack: TypeScript 4.7; PEG.js 0.10 grammar (src/ts/fsl_parser.peg -> src/ts/fsl_parser.ts via `npm run peg`); Vitest (vitest.spec.config.ts / vitest.stoch.config.ts); fast-check for stochastic tests.
meta.hot_files_warning: src/ts/fsl_parser.peg, src/ts/jssm.ts, src/ts/jssm_compiler.ts, src/ts/jssm_types.ts are touched by several tasks. To minimize merge conflicts, land val-phase1 (T1) FIRST, then rebase the other grammar/runtime tasks on it. The cli-* and docs tasks touch disjoint files and are safe to run fully in parallel.

tasks.title: FSL `val` extended state — Phase 1 (scalar core)
tasks.description: Add a machine-level `val` declaration: a named, typed, validated, MUTABLE extended-state variable (the mutable sibling of prop) for the scalar type core (boolean, int, bounded int lo..hi, string, enum(...)), with construction-time type validation and a typed runtime mutation API (set_val). No expression evaluation / assign / where / source-level mutation yet (that is Phase 2). Mirror the existing `property` pipeline end-to-end (grammar -> compiler -> runtime storage/validation -> accessors), adding a parallel `val` track. The one genuinely new piece vs property is a type descriptor (JssmValType) carried grammar->runtime and a validate_val_value function enforcing it at construction and on every write. Initial value resolves: supplied `vals` config option > declared `default` > (required -> throw) > undefined.
tasks.execution: Follow the 12-task plan file step-by-step (Tasks 0-12), but RENAME everything from `var` to `val` mechanically: keyword `val`, JssmValType, JssmValDefinition, val_definition AST key, validate_val_value, _val_keys/_val_types/_val_values/_required_vals fields, accessors val/vals/set_val/known_val/known_vals/val_type, config option `vals`, test files vals.spec.ts / vals.stoch.ts. The plan's grammar/compiler/runtime code blocks are correct apart from the rename. Mirror the live `property_definition` pipeline (jssm_compiler.ts ~line 340; jssm_types.ts ~line 201 JssmPropertyDefinition, ~line 558 config; jssm.ts ~line 471 _property_keys, ~line 867 property_definition loop, ~line 1335 known_props).
acceptance: `jssm.from('val ok : boolean default true; a -> b;').val('ok')` === true
boolean/int/bounded-int/string/enum all parse, validate at construction, and read back
set_val mutates with type validation; unknown-name throws; required/default conflict throws; undeclared supplied-val throws
vals()/known_val/known_vals/val_type accessors; duplicate val-name rejected at compile time
vals.spec.ts + vals.stoch.ts; `npm run vitest-spec` green at 100% coverage; `npm run vet` clean

tasks.title: Overlapping state groups (§19)
tasks.description: Implement overlapping state groups: a state may belong to multiple named groups at once. A complete task-by-task plan exists (see plan path) but predates the jest->vitest migration — refresh the test runner invocations to vitest (vitest.spec.config.ts) before executing. No logical dependency on val, but shares grammar/jssm.ts with T1, so rebase on T1 once it lands.
acceptance: group membership parses + is queryable at runtime
overlap (a state in 2+ groups) works
spec tests cover every branch at 100%
vet clean

tasks.title: Uniform observational-hook registry + introspection (#1357, §12)
tasks.description: Build the generated uniform observational-hook registry keyed by (kind, target, phase), and complete the currently-STUBBED introspection: has_hooks(state) is commented out, leaving dead `hooked_state` styling. Deliver has_hook / hooks_on introspection and wire hooked_state styling off the real registry. Pure observers (veto/mutate stays in source). Independent of val; shares jssm.ts with T1/T2 so rebase after they land.
acceptance: registry enumerates hooks by (kind,target,phase)
has_hook / hooks_on return correct results
hooked_state styling driven by the registry (no longer dead)
100% spec coverage on new branches; vet clean

tasks.title: Factories — F1 (§21)
tasks.description: Implement F1: the `factory` block (literal bindings + seq() + identity targets); the `fsl`/`fslf` template tags + deprecate `sm`; the Factory API; the seed tree + derive() with vectors; cursor semantics. Per the spec F1 makes factories useful from here. NEEDS Phase 1 (T1) merged first (factory bindings reference vals).
acceptance: factory block parses; Factory API constructs machines from literal bindings
derive() with a seed vector is deterministic; cursor semantics per §21.3
fsl/fslf tags work; sm emits a deprecation
100% spec coverage; vet clean

tasks.title: CLI `typegen` verb (§25)
spec_refs: see also notes/superpowers/specs/2026-05-22-fsl-cli-config-design.md and the landed src/ts/cli/config/* for the established CLI patterns
tasks.description: Add `typegen` as a first-class CLI verb DISTINCT from codegen: typegen emits the TYPE SURFACE callers compile against (declarations only — never an implementation), where codegen emits executable source and render emits images. Scaffold the verb following the existing src/ts/cli/ subcommand + config patterns (the fsl-cli-config track already landed). Touches only the CLI dir, so it runs fully in parallel with the grammar/runtime tasks. Richer type output deepens once T1 lands, but the verb + declaration emit can ship against current types.
acceptance: `typegen` registered as its own verb with help text distinguishing it from codegen
emits .d.ts-style declarations for a machine's surface
cli integration + unit specs; vet clean
tasks.conflict_risk: low (cli/ dir is disjoint from grammar/runtime).

tasks.title: Write the FSL manual content (docs)
files: notes/** or src/doc_md/** (whichever the manual build consumes)
tasks.description: Author the FSL manual from the topics + templates specs. Pure documentation — zero code-file collision, fully parallel-safe. Confirm where the manual build sources from (src/doc_md vs notes) before writing, and follow the templates spec's structure.
acceptance: each topic in the topics spec has a written section following the templates
manual builds via the project's doc pipeline without error

tasks.description: Implement the §7 math/standard-library functions as a SELF-CONTAINED pure-function module (no parser/runtime coupling yet — the expression language will call into it in Phase 2). Each function pure, typed, DocBlock'd with @example. Spec + stochastic tests. New files only — fully parallel-safe.

tasks.title: Units — SI prelude (§4.5)
tasks.description: Implement the SI units prelude as a SELF-CONTAINED module: base units + the prefix system (km/ms/μA), derived units via * and /, dimension tracking, auto-convert within a dimension and type-error across. Compile-time phantom types, zero runtime cost. Ship the SI prelude; leave data/imperial/angle/time as opt-in stubs. New files — parallel-safe.
acceptance: base + derived units; prefix system; same-dimension conversion; cross-dimension is a type error
spec tests cover every branch; vet clean

tasks.title: Error model taxonomy (§11)
tasks.description: Implement the §11 error model: the typed error taxonomy (named error kinds, structured fields, messages that name the offending entities). Self-contained type + helper module with tests. If extending jssm_error.ts, keep additions append-only to avoid conflicts. Mostly parallel-safe.
acceptance: the §11 error kinds exist as typed structured errors with named-entity messages
spec tests cover construction + message shape; vet clean
